rationale: Walked R1-R9 building a requirement->code->test map. Every R has an implementation in the changeset, and I ran the AC-named go tests (validate/setup/plugins) live: all PASS; the two tracked copies are byte-identical (diff -q exit 0), the ownership claim is present, 'mindspec complete' count is 0, 'consolidated-round' appears only in a comment. Gate-integrity requirements are genuinely and strongly guarded: R5/R2 by TestMsPanelWorkflow_AllowedCLIExactSet (exactly-four allowlist, buildCommand sole chokepoint, positive enumeration of every mindspec/codex literal, complete-nowhere over whole file); R8 by EmbedsMsPanel + InstallsWorkflowClaudeTargetOnly (embed==install, codex/copilot excluded); R9 by TestWorkflowOwnsClaudeWorkflows. R6/R7 are prose artifacts adequately grep-pinned. The R3 anti-laundering ladder is correct on read: parse_failure -> one same-reviewer re-serialize -> MISSING (never sub); substitution fires only on outcome=='quota_wall'; a retry that hits a quota wall still returns 'missing' — no path replaces a rendered REJECT. The only gaps are the inherent, spec-acknowledged ones (live-LLM JS behavior is Manual-e2e, not go test). Not blockers.
findings: R1: code delivers both tracked copies + plugin source; args destructured; flattenMix reads ONLY args.mix; args.sha never read to set the SHA (advisory display text only, line 461+469); script does all CLI/writes via agent() steps. Tests: AC1 diff -q byte-identity (verified exit 0) + TestWorkflowFiles_EmbedsMsPanel. Falsification sub-clauses 'derives mix elsewhere' / 'sha sets recorded SHA' / 'script execs CLI itself' are guarded by code structure only, not a direct automated test — adequate given the static-analysis + Manual-e2e regime.

R2: registration via buildCommand(CMD_PANEL_CREATE,...) agent step; no hand-typed panel.json. STRONGLY tested — AllowedCLIExactSet positive-enumeration proves 'mindspec panel create' occurs ONLY inside ALLOWED_CLI and buildCommand is the sole chokepoint (defined once), closing the indirection + exact-match-bypass classes.

R3: anti-laundering ladder in runCodexSlot is correct — first parse_failure -> execCodexOnce retry feeding back priorRawStdout (re-serialize, same reviewer_id) -> if not ok, outcome 'missing' (never substituted); quota_wall -> 'quota_wall' (Step-4 eligible). A retry that returns quota_wall still falls to 'missing' (lines 407-410). Confirmed no path replaces a rendered REJECT with a different reviewer or a re-reviewed APPROVE. AUTOMATED COVERAGE GAP: the load-bearing anti-laundering falsification clause has no go test (JS runtime + live LLM) — guarded by code review + the documented, non-CI Manual e2e only. Structurally unguardable in CI; spec scopes it to Manual proof explicitly.

R3b: wrapper persists logPath (.codex.log) via its own Write; codex --sandbox read-only can't write. Tested by presence-grep only (AC5 + AllowedCLIExactSet '.codex.log' assertion); 'log written before/as transcribe' is Manual-e2e.

R4: Step-4 substitutes ONLY outcome=='quota_wall'; claudeSubOnQuota = (args.claude_sub_on_quota===true) fail-closes an absent flag; false -> 'missing' (no fabricated/skipped slot); claude-sub reviewer_id keeps slot id. Branch behavior is Manual-e2e; only 'claude-sub' presence is grep-tested. This was the 9cyu.3 feature-regression fix — code now correct.

R5: returns verifyOutput/tallyOutput verbatim; ALLOWED_CLI is exactly the four verbs; 'mindspec complete' count 0 (verified); no consolidated-round file written ('consolidated-round' only in an explanatory comment). Best-tested requirement — AllowedCLIExactSet is genuinely falsifiable (checked whole-file, not just literals). 'verbatim passthrough' itself is prompt-prose.

R6: ms-panel-run gains 'Runner dispatch' reading `mindspec config show | grep '^runner:'` with claude-code-workflow / claude-code-skills / external branches + capability degrade. Prose artifact; AC8 grep-pins claude-code-workflow, /ms-panel, Slot lens defaults, Launch the panel. Adequate for a prose surface.

R7: four skills-path sections retained + labelled 'claude-code-skills path only' (gated not deleted); Slot lens defaults kept; ms-panel-tally gains a workflow-path note with all four judgment sections (Consolidate, Artifact gates, After a halt, Escape hatch) intact. Grep-pinned by AC8 + tally AC.

R8: embed.go WorkflowFiles() //go:embed workflows/*; claude.go installWorkflows to .claude/workflows only. STRONG — TestWorkflowFiles_EmbedsMsPanel (embed==plugin source via independent os.ReadFile) + TestClaudeSetup_InstallsWorkflowClaudeTargetOnly (install byte-identical; RunCodex/RunCopilot create neither .claude/workflows nor .agents/workflows). FreshSetup/Idempotent counts bumped 14->15. All pass.
R9: OWNERSHIP.yaml adds .claude/workflows/** ; TestWorkflowOwnsClaudeWorkflows asserts attributeDomain -> 'workflow'. Passes. Claim present via AC2 grep (verified).

MINOR (non-blocking): the `node --check .claude/workflows/ms-panel.js` Validation Proof as literally written cannot exit 0 — the file uses top-level await (line 463) and top-level return (line 545), so it is a workflow-runtime function body, not a standalone module. I confirmed the body is valid ESM when wrapped as the runtime evaluates it (async function with args/agent/pipeline injected -> WRAPPED-BODY-VALID-JS-OK). The proof is already flagged 'not a CI gate'; consider noting the wrap so the recorded command matches reality.

MINOR (non-blocking): byte-identity of the two TRACKED copies (.claude/workflows vs plugins/mindspec/workflows) is pinned only by the AC1 shell diff -q, not a go test — the embed/setup tests compare plugins-vs-embed and install-vs-embed. I verified diff -q exits 0. Same dogfood-copy pattern as skills; acceptable.
